title: Rotation angle measuring apparatus and measuring method
abstract: The present invention provides a rotation angle measuring apparatus and method, for measuring a rotation angle of a moving disk relative to a stationary disk; the moving disk can rotate about an axis, and comprises: a reflecting region, having a periodically varying shape; the stationary disk is arranged opposite the moving disk, and comprises: an emitting region and a receiving region, the receiving region being able to form a static electric field with the emitting region, the static electric field being modulated by the reflecting region and hence periodically varying as the moving disk rotates; a magnet is arranged on the moving disk; a first magnetic sensor is arranged on the stationary disk, the first magnetic sensor generating an angle signal under the action of the magnet as the moving disk rotates. An incremental rotation angle of the moving disk is determined on the basis of an output of the receiving region, a period of the static electric field is determined on the basis of an angle signal generated by the first magnetic sensor, and an absolute rotation angle of the moving disk is determined on the basis of the period of the static electric field and the incremental rotation angle. The present invention can determine the absolute rotation angle, with a simple and compact structure, and good measurement precision and robustness.
claims:
Claims Claims 1. A rotation angle measuring apparatus, comprising: a moving disk, capable of rotating about an axis, and comprising: a reflecting region, having a periodically varying shape ; a stationary disk, arranged opposite the moving disk, and comprising: an emitting region,- a receiving region, being able to form a static electric field with the emitting region, the static electric field being modulated by the reflecting region and hence periodically varying as the moving disk rotates; a magnet, arranged on the moving disk; a first magnetic sensor, arranged on the stationary disk, and generating an angle signal under the action of the magnet as the moving disk rotates; a signal processing circuit, which determines an incremental rotation angle of the moving disk on the basis of an output of the receiving region, determines a period of the static electric field on the basis of the angle signal generated by the first magnetic sensor, and determines an absolute rotation angle of the moving disk on the basis of the period of the static electric field and the incremental rotation angle. 2. The apparatus as claimed in claim 1, characterized in that the apparatus also comprises: at least one second magnetic sensor, the second magnetic sensors being arranged on the stationary disk so as to be spaced apart by a set angle, and each generating a pulse signal under the action of the magnet as the moving disk rotates; the signal processing circuit also determining a rotation direction of the moving disk on the basis of signals generated by two magnetic sensors, the two magnetic sensors being two selected from the first magnetic sensor and the at least one 

second magnetic sensor; and determining the number of circles of rotation of the moving disk on the basis of the rotation direction of the moving disk and a pulse signal generated by one of the at least one second magnetic sensor. 3. The apparatus as claimed in claim 2, characterized in that the apparatus comprises two second magnetic sensors; the signal processing circuit determines the rotation direction of the moving disk on the basis of signals generated by the two second magnetic sensors. 4. The apparatus as claimed in claim 3, characterized in that the signal processing circuit causes the first magnetic sensor to be powered by a main power supply only when an absolute rotation angle of the moving disk is being determined. 5. The apparatus as claimed in claim 2, characterized in that the apparatus comprises a second magnetic sensor; the signal processing circuit detects a rising edge and/or a falling edge of a pulse signal generated by the second magnetic sensor, and determines a rotation direction of the moving disk on the basis of an angle signal, corresponding to the rising edge and/or the falling edge respectively, generated by the first magnetic sensor. 6. The apparatus as claimed in claim 5, characterized in that the signal processing circuit causes the first magnetic sensor to be powered only when an angle signal is generated corresponding to the rising edge and/or falling edge, under the condition that the first magnetic sensor is powered by a battery. 7. The apparatus as claimed in any one of claims 1 to 6, characterized in that the magnet is in the form of a disk, radially magnetized, and arranged on an axis of the moving disk. 8. The apparatus as claimed in claim 1 or 7, characterized in that the first magnetic sensor is arranged coaxially with the magnet. 9. The apparatus as claimed in claim 2 or 7, characterized in that the at least one second magnetic sensor is arranged to be spaced apart from the magnet by a set distance in a radial direction. 10. The apparatus as claimed in claim 9, characterized in that each second magnetic sensor generates a pulse with a duty cycle of 50% in one circle of rotation of the moving disk. 11. The apparatus as claimed in claim 1, characterized in that the moving disk also comprises another reflecting region, wherein the reflecting region and the other reflecting region complement one another in shape and are arranged differentially; the stationary disk also comprises another receiving region, wherein the receiving region and the other receiving region are arranged on two sides of the emitting region, the other receiving region can form another static electric field with the emitting region, and the other static electric field is modulated by the other reflecting region and hence periodically varies as the moving disk rotates; the signal processing circuit determines an incremental rotation angle of the moving disk on the basis of the difference between outputs of the receiving region and the other receiving region. 12. A rotation angle measuring method, wherein the rotation angle means a rotation angle of a moving disk relative to a stationary disk; the moving disk can rotate about an axis, and comprises: a reflecting region, having a periodically varying shape; the stationary disk is arranged opposite the moving disk, and comprises: an emitting region and a receiving region, the receiving region being able to form a static electric field 

with the emitting region, the static electric field being modulated by the reflecting region and hence periodically varying as the moving disk rotates; a magnet is arranged on the moving disk; a first magnetic sensor is arranged on the stationary disk, the first magnetic sensor generating an angle signal under the action of the magnet as the moving disk rotates; the method comprises: determining an incremental rotation angle of the moving disk on the basis of an output of the receiving region, determining a period of the static electric field on the basis of an angle signal generated by the first magnetic sensor, and determining an absolute rotation angle of the moving disk on the basis of the period of the static electric field and the incremental rotation angle. 13. The method as claimed in claim 12, characterized in that at least one second magnetic sensor is also arranged on the static disk, the second magnetic sensors being arranged to be spaced apart by a set angle, and each generating a pulse signal under the action of the magnet as the moving disk rotates; the method also comprises: determining a rotation direction of the moving disk on the basis of signals generated by two magnetic sensors, the two magnetic sensors being two selected from the first magnetic sensor and the at least one second magnetic sensor; and determining the number of circles of rotation of the moving disk on the basis of the rotation direction of the moving disk and a pulse signal generated by one of the at least one second magnetic sensor. 14. The method as claimed in claim 13, characterized in that two second magnetic sensors are arranged on the stationary disk; the step of determining a rotation direction of the moving disk on the basis of signals generated by two magnetic sensors comprises: 

 determining the rotation direction of the moving disk on the basis of the order of signals generated by the two second magnetic sensors. 15. The method as claimed in claim 13, characterized in that one second magnetic sensor is arranged on the stationary disk; the step of determining a rotation direction of the moving disk on the basis of signals generated by two magnetic sensors comprises : detecting a rising edge and/or a falling edge of a pulse signal generated by the second magnetic sensor, and determining a rotation direction of the moving disk on the basis of an angle signal, corresponding to the rising edge and/or the falling edge respectively, generated by the first magnetic sensor. 16. The method as claimed in any one of claims 13 to 15, characterized in that an absolute rotation angle of the moving disk is determined only when a main power supply is turned on; and an incremental rotation angle and the number of circles of rotation of the moving disk are determined continuously. 17. The method as claimed in claim 12, characterized in that the moving disk also comprises another reflecting region, wherein the reflecting region and the other reflecting region complement one another in shape and are arranged differentially; the stationary disk also comprises another receiving region, wherein the receiving region and the other receiving region are arranged on two sides of the emitting region, the other receiving region can form another static electric field with the emitting region, and the other static electric field is modulated by the other reflecting region and hence periodically varies as the moving disk rotates; the step of determining an incremental rotation angle of the moving disk on the basis of an output of the receiving region comprises: 

 determining the incremental rotation angle of the moving disk on the basis of the difference between outputs of the receiving region and the other receiving region.
